summary: In a 1996 high school graduation speech, Steve Jobs advises students to follow their intuition and avoid the stifling concept of a traditional 'career.' He emphasizes that creativity comes from connecting diverse life experiences, that the journey itself is the true reward, and that an awareness of mortality should drive one to live fully and avoid the regret of inaction.

principles.rationale: Intuitive feelings about what you want to do with your life are real. If you listen to them rather than the practical objections of others, they can blossom into something magical.
principles.source_quote: These feelings are very real, and if nurtured can blossom into something wonderful and magical.

principles.statement: Creativity requires diverse, seemingly unrelated experiences.
principles.rationale: You cannot connect dots if you travel the same path as everyone else. You must invest in uncharted paths and trust they will connect later.
principles.source_quote: Creativity equals connecting previously unrelated experiences and insights that others don’t see.

principles.rationale: Work shouldn't be separate from the rest of your life. If you are passionate about your life and your work, they become one, which is a much better way to live.
principles.source_quote: Make your avocation your vocation. Make what you love your work.

principles.rationale: Reaching the end goal (the pot of gold) isn't the actual reward; the process of getting there is what truly matters.
principles.source_quote: The journey is the reward. People think that you’ve made it when you’ve gotten to the end of the rainbow and got the pot of gold. But they’re wrong. The reward is in the crossing the rainbow.

mental_models.name: The Life Rainbow
mental_models.description: Visualizing life as a brief, finite arc across the sky between birth and death. Acknowledging this mortality shouldn't be frightening; it should be the ultimate motivator to live fully and 'blaze' while you can.
mental_models.example: Jobs reflects on the endpoints of birth and death, stating that knowing his arc will fall makes him want to 'blaze while I am in the sky.'

mental_models.name: Mistakes vs. Regrets
mental_models.description: A lens for evaluating the past. Mistakes are actions you took that failed or caused hurt, but if your intent was pure, they are ultimately enriching. Regrets, conversely, are the things you failed to do.
mental_models.example: Jobs notes that mistakes are enriching, whereas his real regret is inaction, like not kissing Nancy Kinniman in high school.

heuristics: Walk alone near sunset and look at the sky to remember and nurture your intuitive feelings.
Seek out new dimensions of yourself—especially those that carry a romantic scent.

signature_quotes.text: Creativity equals connecting previously unrelated experiences and insights that others don’t see.
signature_quotes.context: Explaining why one must seek out diverse experiences, like his calligraphy class, to be creative.

signature_quotes.text: Don’t be a career. The enemy of most dreams and intuitions, and one of the most dangerous and stifling concepts ever invented by humans, is the 'Career.'
signature_quotes.context: Warning graduates against separating their work from their life's passion.
Explaining that the process of achieving something is more valuable than the final payoff.

signature_quotes.text: To know my arc will fall makes me want to blaze while I am in the sky.
signature_quotes.context: Discussing how the awareness of mortality motivates him.

signature_quotes.text: Regrets are most often things you didn’t do, and wish you did.
signature_quotes.context: Differentiating between making mistakes and having regrets.

anti_patterns.description: Pursuing a 'Career' as a structured progression of stages.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It creates a false separation between work and life, stifling dreams and intuitions. People seek 'safe harbors' like law or accounting, only to wake up years later and realize the heavy price they paid.

anti_patterns.description: Believing the reward is the 'pot of gold' at the end.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Once you achieve the end goal, you realize it isn't the actual reward, and you are left simply looking for another rainbow to cross. The true reward is the crossing itself.
